title: UI-081: added scope selectors for library, catalogue, and analytics, made export config filtering...
summary: the import/export workflow UI was named and wired as Library-only even though Catalogue and Analytics need the same export, staging, and preview workflow shell for future LLM review work. Outcome: added scope selectors for library, catalogue, and analytics, made export config filtering and import staging/preview calls use the selected scope, allowed staged JSON/JSONL preview folders for the three workflow scopes, and kept Library source-write apply buttons disabled for non-Library scopes.
body:
## UI Rule Log 2026-05-05 / UI-081

- status: adopted
- route: `/studio/export/`, `/studio/import/`
- issue: the import/export workflow UI was named and wired as Library-only even though Catalogue and Analytics need the same export, staging, and preview workflow shell for future LLM review work.
- triage: shared workflow shell
- reasoning: scope-specific config shapes and write actions can be designed later, but the Studio route should already separate workflow scope from route implementation. The page should not fail just because a future scope has no enabled configs yet.
- outcome: added scope selectors for `library`, `catalogue`, and `analytics`, made export config filtering and import staging/preview calls use the selected scope, allowed staged JSON/JSONL preview folders for the three workflow scopes, and kept Library source-write apply buttons disabled for non-Library scopes.
- files changed:
  - `studio/export/index.md`
  - `studio/import/index.md`
  - `assets/studio/js/data-export.js`
  - `assets/studio/js/data-import.js`
  - `assets/studio/css/studio.css`
  - `assets/studio/data/studio_config.json`
  - `scripts/docs/docs_import.py`
  - `scripts/docs/docs_management_server.py`
  - `_docs/library-export.md`
  - `_docs/library-import.md`
  - `_docs/scripts-docs-import.md`
  - `_docs/scripts-docs-management-server.md`
- local verification:
  - open Library import/export with default scope and confirm existing Library behavior still loads
  - open `?scope=catalogue` and `?scope=analytics` and confirm the shell stays ready with scope-specific status text
  - run the Docs import service test covering Catalogue staging preview
effect: scope-specific config shapes and write actions can be designed later, but the Studio route should already separate workflow scope from route implementation.
verification: open Library import/export with default scope and confirm existing Library behavior still loads
open `?scope=catalogue` and `?scope=analytics` and confirm the shell stays ready with scope-specific status text
run the Docs import service test covering Catalogue staging preview
